neighbors.name: Joni E. McLin
neighbors.claims: Joni E. McLin is listed as the owner of a parcel in Cochise County (PIN 106-47-148) according to county property records [Cochise County Treasurer](https://parcelinquirytreasurer.cochise.az.gov). No references to her involvement in local government, public meetings, or development issues were found in available public records or news archives (suggesting she has not taken a visible public stance) [Cochise County Board of Supervisors Minutes – Search](https://cochise.az.gov/AgendaCenter) [Herald/Review Archives – Search](https://www.myheraldreview.com/search/).

approach_recommendations.engage: Begin with a straightforward, informational outreach, as McLin has no known public positions. A polite introduction emphasizing transparency about the project and addressing common concerns (e.g., property values, privacy) may help engage her interest and build trust.

neighbors.name: Grant D. Miles
neighbors.claims: Grant D. Miles is a long-time Cochise County landowner, approximately 74 years old, who resides in the Cochise, AZ area (Big Draw Road) near his property [Public Records Database](https://www.menstoppingviolence.org/people/grant-miles/). He has no recorded public statements or involvement in local development disputes or energy projects in the county’s available records [Cochise County Board of Supervisors – Minutes Search](https://cochise.az.gov/AgendaCenter) [Willcox Range News – Archives Search](https://www.myheraldreview.com/search/).

approach_recommendations.engage: As an older landowner with deep local ties but no public stance, a peer-to-peer approach is recommended. Having a respected local farmer or rancher introduce the project — focusing on aspects like land stewardship and minimal impact on agriculture — could help gain Miles’s receptivity.

neighbors.name: Rebekah & Ronald L. Mourfield
neighbors.claims: Rebekah and Ronald L. Mourfield jointly own a parcel in Cochise County (PIN 106-48-002) as listed in county property records [Cochise County Treasurer](https://parcelinquirytreasurer.cochise.az.gov). There is no indication in public sources (county meeting minutes, local news, or community organizations) that either Rebekah or Ronald Mourfield has taken any public position or active role regarding development or energy projects [Cochise County Planning & Zoning Minutes – Search](https://cochise.az.gov/AgendaCenter) [Herald/Review News – Search](https://www.myheraldreview.com/search/).

approach_recommendations.engage: The Mourfields have no known advocacy history, so initial engagement should be neutral and informative. A community open house or an invitation to a site tour could be effective, allowing them to ask questions in a casual setting and reassuring them that their quality of life (quiet, safety, property value) will be respected.

neighbors.name: Qwest Corporation
neighbors.claims: Qwest Corporation is a major telecommunications utility (an incumbent local exchange carrier) providing telephone and internet services across Arizona and other states [Grokipedia](https://grokipedia.com/page/Qwest_Corporation). This company holds ownership of parcel PIN 106-48-152, likely for infrastructure purposes, and does not participate in community matters as an individual neighbor would. (**See organizational profile for Qwest Corporation in ORG table for further details**.)

approach_recommendations.engage: Coordinate through formal channels with Qwest’s regional liaison or right-of-way department. Ensure any project plans account for existing telecom infrastructure on the parcel and address technical requirements rather than community persuasion.

overview_summary: All identified neighbors around the site have no publicly documented stance on development or clean energy, with their positions classified as "unknown." None of the profiled individuals holds a high-profile role in local affairs, indicating a low concentration of community influence among these neighbors.
